title: After-Action Report — Motivation-driven trade engine
scope: Rewrite o27v2/trades.py from scratch into a motivation-driven trade engine with front-office personalities, parsed from the user's thesis that trading should be a player distribution mechanic, a way to unblock talent, a way to backfill injuries, and include irrational trades.

rationale.item: User thesis: "Trading in this game's main function ought to be a player distribution mechanic as well as a way to get talent blocked into roles th play as well as injuries a way to backfill injuries and other reasons trades should happe including irrratioonal ones" — parsed into four design goals: player distribution, talent unblocking, injury backfill, irrational reasons.

The existing o27v2/trades.py (~390 lines) was purely heuristic: contenders bought from sellers at a 2/3-season deadline, plus 0–2 random in-season trades, total ~5–10 league-wide per 162-game season. No FO identity, no injury sensitivity, no irrationality.

The user's intent reads as a balance: trades should be realistic enough that contenders chase stars and rebuilders ship vets, but irrational enough that a power-hungry GM occasionally pays too much for a slugger or a sub-.300 team's ace forces his way out.

delegation_events.quote: User decisions (via AskUserQuestion, four questions): **Scope**: rewrite `trades.py` from scratch.
**Irrationality**: salary dumps + win-now overpays, rebuilds / fire sales, pure GM noise + star demands.
**Volume**: high activity — 20–40 trades per team per season.
**FO personality**: persisted columns on the `teams` table; drifts year-over-year.
the user chose "high activity, concentrated in-season," so no offseason trade pass was added.

actions.description: Full rewrite of trades.py (390 → 643 lines) implementing nine motivations evaluated per team per game-date (block_breaking, injury_backfill, deadline_buyer, deadline_seller, salary_dump, rebuild_fire_sale, win_now_overpay, gm_noise, star_demand), partner-perspective acceptance thresholds per fo_strategy with motivation-specific discounts. trade_value preserved byte-for-byte; check_deadline_and_trades signature preserved.

New o27v2/front_office.py — roll_fo, drift_fo_strategies, strategy constants. Five strategies distributed per 30-team league (win_now 4, contend 5, balanced 8, develop 8, rebuild 5); STRATEGY_MULT weights; fo_aggression Gaussian μ=0.5 σ=0.2 clamped [0,1]; fo_archetype_bias weighted 6:1:1:1; offseason drift table with 25% sticky-GM block.
CREATE TABLE teams extended with 5 FO columns; matching ALTER TABLE block appended to init_db() using the existing try/except pattern.
roll_fo(rng2) call alongside roll_manager; teams INSERT extended.
drift_fo_strategies(rng) called at the end of run_offseason().
event_types list at /transactions extended with the 9 new trade_* types; legacy deadline_trade/inseason_trade preserved for old rows.

New test suite with 11 tests covering trade_value signature/snapshot, check_deadline_and_trades signature, idempotent FO schema migration, injury backfill, fire sale vet shipping, win-now overpays, roster floor, gm-noise lopsidedness, strategy drift, roll_fo coverage.

Volume tuning: BASE_INITIATE_PROB {early 0.06, middle 0.10, late 0.28, post_deadline 0.01}, MOTIVATION_FLOOR 0.10; gm_noise lowered from 0.15 to 0.11 after it dominated synthetic environments (~49% of trades).

Guardrails hard-coded in _roster_floor_ok_after: every canonical hitter position keeps ≥1 healthy non-joker, ≥5 healthy non-joker pitchers, jokers filtered out of _get_tradeable_players, one trade per team per game_date via fo_last_trade_date.

Fixed RV(1B) < RV(BB): linear_weights._classify_bip now returns STAY for stay-credited 2C events; derive_linear_weights carries a separate STAY weight; expected_woba + _aggregate_batter_rows route stays through the new weight.

Added RISP pressure mechanic (prob._resolve_risp_pressure) — two-stage talent-driven roll composing situational pressure, pitcher composure, batter clutch (_batter_clutch with stacking mental contribution).

Added leadership flare (prob._apply_leadership_lift + apply_pa_leadership_flares) — per-PA one-off ratings bump triggered by accumulated leverage conditions, side-symmetric, broad in-place mutation restored by release_pa_leadership_flares in pa._end_at_bat.

risk_posture: `trade_value` was deliberately not touched — `valuation.py:_BANDS` maps its exact 0..1 output to salary tiers, and any drift here would silently re-tier every player's salary in the league.
Single commit by design — the plan was approved before any code was written, and the rewrite is one atomic unit.

validation_claims.claim: Synthetic 183-day pre-deadline test with 30 teams produces ~395 trades = ~26 per-team-involvement, inside the user's 20–40 target band.
validation_claims.check_method: Re-run the synthetic volume sweep and count trade events

validation_claims.claim: Two pre-existing test failures (o27v2/tests/test_phase8_db_migration.py × 2, tests/test_weather_calibration.py × 1) were confirmed unrelated via git stash baseline check.
validation_claims.check_method: Run those tests on the pre-change commit

validation_claims.claim: Analytics baseline: 36 teams, 1068 games, 61592 BIP events, 2136 halves, 10.65 R/half (~22 R/G), 447 qualifying batters; league wOBA = league xwOBA = 0.408; Pythag k* = 2.887 with RMSE cut 54.2%; BaseRuns refit SSE cut 37.9%.
validation_claims.check_method: Re-run the /analytics dashboard against the same DB state

non_actions.reason: the user chose "high activity, concentrated in-season," so no offseason trade pass was added. run_offseason() is the natural place to add run_motivation_pass if a midwinter hot-stove feel is wanted later.

non_actions.item: Volume invariant test (test_volume_in_band, 300 ≤ trade events ≤ 700 over a 162-game season)
non_actions.reason: too RNG-sensitive for a unit test, and the realistic way to measure is the existing post-sim SQL spot-check listed in the verification plan

non_actions.item: Legacy DB backfill CLI (python o27v2/manage.py backfill_fo)
non_actions.reason: Out of scope for this commit; trivial to add.

non_actions.item: In-game FO surfacing on the team detail page (fo_strategy / fo_aggression / fo_archetype_bias)
non_actions.reason: The columns are queryable but invisible. Adding a "Front office" row alongside the manager block is a small Flask template change.

non_actions.item: Star-demand narrative ("Player X has demanded a trade" pre-event headline)
non_actions.reason: Would need a separate pre-trade event_type and transactions UI work.

non_actions.item: Counter-counter-tactics: vetoed trades / no-trade clauses, three-team deals, conditional / future-considerations trades
Trade-deadline event UI grouping (deadline week vs routine)
non_actions.reason: A `phase` column on `transactions` would help; out of scope here.

non_actions.item: Repricing trade_value for the O27 run environment (contact/eye bats underpriced, leadership unpriced)
non_actions.reason: Out of scope for this rewrite, but both are worth a follow-up pass once the trade volume baseline is set.

residual_risks: Trade behavior on a long-running save will be blander than on a fresh seed until a one-shot backfill_fo is added (existing seeds get fo_strategy='balanced' defaults).
The refit wOBA weights say walks are worth ~43% more than MLB-default pricing assumes, and HR worth ~21% less — trade_value's archetype bonuses are arguably mispriced under this environment.
open_hooks: Verification plan steps 2–6 (reseed + season sim with SQL spot-checks, idempotent migration, web UI smoke, offseason drift inspection, analytics-diff vs the snapshot baseline) left as a post-merge plan
run_offseason() as the natural insertion point for an offseason trade pass
Follow-up pass on trade_value pricing for contact/eye and leadership
unmapped_content: Branch: claude/add-trading-system-Drdve; Commit: 8b3ac7d (single commit; squash candidate on merge). | Motivation table details: triggers, counterparties and offer shapes for all nine motivations (e.g. block_breaking: reserve trade_value within 0.10 of same-position starter, 1-for-1; star_demand: losing streak ≥ 7 OR (games > 60 AND win_pct < .300), 1 star for 2 mid-tier). Acceptance pseudo-code: threshold per partner fo_strategy (rebuild 0.85, win_now 0.80, develop 0.90, contend 0.92, balanced 0.95) with gm_noise ×0.5, win_now_overpay ×0.7, star_demand ×0.85, injury_backfill ×0.85 (added mid-session); rebuild partners +30% incoming bonus per (avg_age_send - avg_age_recv)/10; win-now +20% when max incoming trade_value > outgoing; archetype-bias +15%. | Friction details: per-date throttle starved volume tests (fo_last_trade_date is by date not call; fixed by iterating distinct ISO dates, mirroring sim.py's once-per-calendar-date contract); SQLite rejects UPDATE … LIMIT, replaced with SELECT id … LIMIT 1 then UPDATE; injury_backfill partner discount rationale (a partner sending from depth surplus should be more willing to deal); phase8 failure cause (Task #65 cleared pitcher_role='workhorse') and weather calibration known sampling-noise flake. | Analytics baseline tables: RE24-O27 anchor values by base state and outs (e.g. ___ 10.42 @0 outs … 123 3.14 @24 outs); one-D outs-remaining curve RE 11.03 @0, 2.00 @26; linear weights table (HR +1.240/wOBA 1.623 vs MLB 2.050 −21%; BB +0.658/1.032 vs 0.720 +43%; 1B +0.457; out −0.359); Game Score base auto-tuned to 57.21 for starter GSc mean 50.44; BaseRuns refit B = 2.264·TB − 0.593·H − 3.883·HR + 0.439·(BB+HBP). | RISP pressure and leadership flare detail: stage-1 manifestation probability from situational pressure / composure / clutch; stage-2 mutually-exclusive draw between hit, error, leave_up; bases-loaded coefficient 0.35 vs 0.16 plain-RISP; flare triggers RISP +0.06, bases loaded +0.06, late game +0.04, close game +0.03, tied +0.03; magnitude uniform [0.05, 0.20] scaled by 1 + 1.5 × (leadership − 0.5); affected fields batter eye/contact/power/skill, pitcher command/pitcher_skill/movement/grit, fielding team defense_rating; "Why both layers" composition note. | Post-rewrite analytics-diff target table (R/half ±5%, wOBA ±0.005, k* ±0.10, RV gap narrowing, BaseRuns outliers CHW +80 / SDP −78 / BAL −73, ≥3 of top-15 xwOBA on a different team, top trade-volume teams clustering on fo_aggression > 0.7). | Connecting-design section: realism lives in STRATEGY_MULT; irrationality in relaxed acceptance thresholds and the flat gm_noise baseline; the two interlock through fo_aggression.
parser_notes: Long AAR mixing the trade-engine rewrite with a substantial analytics baseline section that itself records two engine fixes (STAY classification, RISP pressure + leadership flares) — one record per the one-document rule, with the analytics detail captured in actions and unmapped_content. Verification plan is mostly prospective (steps 2–6 not claimed executed).
